title: Viewing Habits
wikitext:
[]: Viewing Habits             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Mon Jun 01, 2009 7:29 am I'm curious over the sanity of some of the clientele here ...as well as their basic level of love for the series... So, thought I'd ask- what are everybody's viewing habits for NGE? Every few months I watch the series from start to finish (aka ep24)- I might be bored, in need of some inspiration, or just feel that itch. I normally watch in sets of two episodes per sitting. I've never endured a marathon run from start to finish. I always start from the beginning and never skip any episodes, not even those two. Out of respect. ^ Anybody have a similar pattern, or is this not geeky enough?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 01, 2009 7:41 am When I first read that post, I thought I was reading a poem or a riddle. But then I realized that not even I could do that bad. xP I haven't seen the series in well over a year probably, maybe the occasional random episode here and there. I once attempted to marathon it once, but I made the mistake of starting at 8:00pm on a school night and only made it about halfway through the series. The first time I watched it was on [Adult Swim], so I missed a lot of episodes when I couldn't make it to a tv but for the most part was in order, until the series ended and they started playing it over again, and that's when things got a bit confusing. But when I bought the Perfect Collection on DVD from some guy in the middle of the mall I was able to watch the series from beginning to end.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jun 01, 2009 8:08 am Last time I watched the series all the way through was about 2 years ago. I watched Rebuild recently, and now that I'm getting into Evangelion again I'll probably watch the series again sometime soon. Inevitably though, I'll run out of things to get excited about and move on to something else for a few months, and then eventually come back to Evangelion again. I've got a sort of "wheel of fandom" going on.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jun 01, 2009 8:38 am I haven't watched a full episode in three years, though I did see EoE and Rebirth in '07 Sachi_13 wrote: When I first read that post, I thought I was reading a poem or a riddle.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Jun 01, 2009 10:10 am For the past few years it has been an annual thing. I always watch the whole series (Platinum Director's Cut) except for the last two episodes. After 24, I go straight into "End of Evangelion". I consider episodes 25 and 26 to be sort of like an appendix, which I might watch randomly months after the fact. I tend to watch four or five episodes a day, although last time I started at 5 AM and watched until I was done. I was quite high for the entire thing, and it was like a dream. ; I couldn't stop thinking about Eva for a week after that, and... actually that's not long before I joined this forum, haha. The combination of the magical herb and an intense NGE marathon is one of my favourite ways to indulge myself (up there with bubble baths). It's a dreamy blissathon. @_@ Mmmmmmm~ My next scheduled marathon will most likely come soon after the second Rebuild movie's fansub surfaces. >_> <_< I tend to marathon because I find I really appreciate character development and all the nuance more. By surrendering all of my attention, I gain so much more. That's why I can only do it once a year, haha. X_x If I watch spread out over a week or two, the impact is lessened.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 01, 2009 10:50 am I load up random scenes on an as-needed basis for fandom stuff, and am so overexposed to screenshots and scripts, I don't really sit down and watch the show that often. Usually just on the really rare occasions I show it to someone else, or feel the urge to watch some particular episode.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 01, 2009 11:00 am I don't have a schedule or anything, but I do review it quite often. I'm actually in the middle of a watch-through right now for the purposes of refreshing myself for a wiki article (though I've been sidetracked by RL quite a bit lately...starting a business will do that to you). I've been watching just one episode at a time, but when I watch the show solely for entertainment purposes I tend to watch related chunks in one sitting (1-7, 8-13, 14-20, 21-26, EoE).

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Mon Jun 01, 2009 11:38 am due to my short attention span, it is difficult for me to just sit and watch a show without moving around but that doesn't it is impossible for me to do so. nowadays, I watch an episode or two just to get a 'feel' for a show and then later on, I watch the whole series when I have time.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jun 01, 2009 11:58 am Nowadays I might watch a few select scenes from an episode or watch the last two TV episodes and watch few select scenes from EoE. Mostly when I watch EoE I watch the second half mostly. Or for fun once in a while I'll watch episode one and then cut to EoE. When I originally saw Evangelion(on youtube) I marathoned it. I didn't see all the episodes in one sitting. Maybe about 5 or 6 at the time(I had a lot of spare time >_>; )

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Jun 01, 2009 12:08 pm every once in a while i'll watch a random episode or a fight from one of the movies. I mostly get my fill from amvs and parodies

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Jun 01, 2009 12:41 pm I watch the seires like three or four times in my life. I finish the hole boxset like in one or two days after X-Mass of 08 since I was hook to it (I was also reading Watchmen and watching House at the same time also). When it was on Adult Swim it finish it expect for the last two episodes which I did got the chance to finish until I got the boxset.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 01, 2009 2:32 pm I've not watched the series through since the first time, 5+ years ago, though I did watch the DC epsodes when they came out on DVD, so that counted as a 2nd pass. I watched episodes 5-8 earlier this year, to refresh my memory before going back to the current fic, but episodes 1-4 are etched into my brain from the Commentary project. EoE I watched twice, at similar remove. With the scripts available, I find I have too much of the series committed to memory for there to be rewatch value.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Jun 01, 2009 2:34 pm I watch EoE like three times and Rebuild once (It will be twice or 3rd once I get it on DVD). I seen Death & Rebirth like one since it was just one big flash back and nothing special or anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 01, 2009 3:19 pm I watch random episodes* (including Rebuild and EoE) when the mood strikes me, but I haven't watched the full series since watching it the first time through. I hope to rewatch it again in order when I can find the time, considering I (finally) have all my volumes of Platinum (plus Rebuild feature films, on my computer AND on DVD =3 ). *I tend to watch something from the action arc, ep22', or just whatever episode I feel like at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Mon Jun 01, 2009 3:27 pm I watch different episodes every now and then. I watched Rebuild 1.0 a couple of times on the internet but the movie got removed from the site it was hosted on. I haven't seen Death or Rebirth.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Jun 01, 2009 3:45 pm Nowadays, I really only watch it when I show it to friends, which isn't often. Other than that, I haven't watched it for a long time.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jun 01, 2009 4:11 pm Watched it through 3 or 4 times, then all episodes while making notes (Subject (eg, Core), Episode, Principles, Notes). Since then, when I have a little free time I'll bring it up and continue from the last stopping point (I've ripped the DC to MP4 so it's available on any screen in the house) - kind of All Eva All the Time. It might take months to get through the whole series like this, I'm currently on Episode 1 of the second pass. I've watched anything from 5 to 45 minutes at a sitting. Specific episodes I've watched several times (Ritsuko's Big Reveal, for example).

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Mon Jun 01, 2009 5:40 pm Evangelion is not a series where I would sit down a watch a episode for fun since I already deal with all the Mind Fuck moments in the series and I don't want to deal with it again.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Jun 01, 2009 6:38 pm I've never seen the whole series. I have seen every episode plus EOE, with the exeption of the recap episode. I just can't stand recap episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jun 01, 2009 7:03 pm The recap episodes are teh über boring. There are some other episodes I would probably skip under normal circumstances (like 10 and 13), but I can't bring myself to because of
